channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: TCS HACKATHON WINNER 2026 🏆
item.description: Proud Moment for Me! 🏆 I am thrilled to share that I have won the AI Track at Hackathon 2026 (AI & Cyber Security Challenge) organized at Maharana Pratap Institute of Technology, Gorakhpur,...

item.title: The 'Code' was Never the Core: Reimagining the DLC with AI
item.description: Based on my recent experience trying to integrate AI into my team’s Development Life Cycle (DLC), I’ve hit some unexpected roadblocks. It wasn't just about technical hurdles; it was a mix of...

item.title: Contrarian View: 2026 Startups Should Skip Vercel for Next.js 15 – Use Cloudflare Pages for 40% Lower Hosting Costs
item.description: By Q2 2026, Vercel’s standard Next.js 15 hosting tier will cost startups an average of $0.08 per GB of bandwidth and $0.12 per serverless function execution—40% more than Cloudflare Pages’...

item.title: Inside Graviton4: How AWS's New Chip Cuts EC2 Costs by 20% for Compute-Heavy Workloads
item.description: Inside Graviton4: How AWS's New Chip Cuts EC2 Costs by 20% for Compute-Heavy Workloads AWS recently launched Graviton4, its fourth-generation custom silicon chip built specifically for EC2...

item.title: The Augmented Builder: How AI Reimagined the Product-to-Code Pipeline
item.description: I've spent the last few weeks building Brainwake, a mental agility app. While the goal was to create a tool for cognitive focus, the real discovery was the "new normal" of the build process. In...

item.title: When Retries Turn Hostile — How Control Logic Kills Production Systems
item.description: "Your retries are killing us." A service team received this message from a downstream dependency during an outage. The upstream API was timing out, so naturally, the client retried. 3 times, 5...

item.title: The Next-Generation Developer Path: From First Line of Code to First Job
item.description: TL;DR — Five stages turn a software creator into a job-ready developer: experimenting with AI, attending a beginner-friendly MLH hackathon for structured immersion, building solo projects,...

item.title: AI Isn’t Creating Jobs. It’s Replacing Jobs With Tasks - Mr Chandravanshi
item.description: Why platforms like RentAHuman.ai feel like employment but behave differently Open the app. A task appears. “Take a photo of this shelf.” You complete it. Another task appears. You complete that...

item.title: This accessory can snap a Steam Controller to your phone — or almost anything else
item.description: Valve's new Steam Controller goes on sale on Monday for $99, and accessories-maker Mechanism will be ready. As far as we know, Mechanism's new Basegrip is the very first way to attach a Steam...

item.title: A No-Build Markdown Site for Study Notes (or any docs)
item.description: I needed a way to study a stack of notes I had written for a job interview. Eight markdown files, plus some code examples, plus a couple of reference pages. I wanted to read them in a browser with...

item.title: Debug MCP Like Network Tab: Seeing Every Tool Call in Real Time
item.description: You triggered a tool call from Cursor, something went wrong, and the only feedback was silence—or a vague error. The server is running, the tool is registered, but you have no idea what actually...

item.title: Players from the NBA, NFL, and MLB call for a ban on betting ‘unders’
item.description: The unions backing professional NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, and MLS players are calling on the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to ban prediction market platforms from allowing users to bet on...

item.title: Severe Linux Copy Fail security flaw uncovered using AI scanning help
item.description: Nearly every Linux distribution released since 2017 is currently vulnerable to a security bug called "Copy Fail" that allows any user to give themselves administrator privileges. The exploit,...

item.title: Dreame’s rocket-powered car can do 0–60 in 0.9 seconds because you can just say things now
item.description: When I first heard that a vacuum company had released a rocket-powered electric vehicle with physically impossible-sounding performance specs, I immediately thought James Dyson was up to his old...

item.title: We just got a new reason to believe the Trump phone is real(ish)
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This time we've discovered a little more evidence...

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

item.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
item.description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore....

item.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
item.description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable...

item.title: Aurzen’s tiny trifold projector is almost 40 percent off right now
item.description: Not all trifolds are created equal — just ask our own Allison Johnson, who recently spent time with Samsung’s ill-fated Galaxy phone. Aurzen’s Zip projector is the kind of trifold gadget we can...

item.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of two cybercrime groups that are carrying out "rapid, high-impact attacks" operating almost within the confines of SaaS environments, while leaving minimal...

item.title: Some of Xteink’s credit card-sized e-readers are losing their best feature
item.description: The Xteink X4 and X3 are excellent alternatives to Kindles and Kobos if you want an e-reader that's as easy to slip into your pocket - just not out of the box. Both devices come with stock...

item.title: Pentagon strikes classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia — but not Anthropic
item.description: The Pentagon has struck deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, Elon Musk's xAI, and the startup Reflection, allowing the agency to use their AI tools in classified settings,...

item.title: The Verge’s 2026 Mother’s Day gift guide
item.description: Whether it's managing a busy home or looking out for everyone around them, moms spend a lot of time every day caring for others. Mother's Day, May 10th, is an opportunity to return the favor, so...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Target Asian Governments, NATO State, Journalists, and Activists
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new China-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defense sectors across South, East, and Southeast Asia, along with one European...

item.title: My $5K smart bed needs to shut the hell up
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your...

item.title: The Download: a new Christian phone network, and debugging LLMs
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn...

item.title: A Ransomware Negotiator Was Working for a Ransomware Gang
item.description: Someone pleaded guilty to secretly working for a ransomware gang as he negotiated ransomware payments for clients.

item.title: Top Five Sales Challenges Costing MSPs Cybersecurity Revenue
item.description: The managed security services market is projected to grow from $38.31 billion in 2025 to $69.16 billion by 2030[1], with cybersecurity being the fastest-growing sector[2]. Despite this...

item.title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
item.description: Smack dab between Australia and South America, the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) research vessel Rainier is currently on a mission to map more than 8,000 square...

item.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Get 4-Year Sentences in BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the sentencing of two cybersecurity professionals to four years each in prison for their role in facilitating BlackCat ransomware attacks...
